response.justification: Bogdan brings 16+ years of professional software development with a strong TypeScript/Node.js core that directly matches the primary stack, and his current role at re:cinq explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development — a precise match for the AI agent requirement. His testing culture is exceptional: TDD advocacy, open-source testing libraries, community workshops, and cross-role application of testing practices. The main gaps are the complete absence of Nest.js (the primary backend framework) and Kotlin/Java (a required secondary stack), and React depth is unclear beyond a single Optilyz mention. These are learnable gaps given his breadth, making him a strong interview candidate with caveats to probe.
strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple production roles spanning direct mail automation, fintech, and high-throughput ad-tech microservices
Current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) work at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement
Exceptional testing culture: TDD advocate, author of open-source D testing libraries, and organizer of community testing workshops and refactoring dojos
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere in the resume, yet it is the explicit primary backend framework for the role
No Kotlin or Java experience listed despite both being named required secondary technologies
React exposure appears limited to a single role at Optilyz; frontend depth and component architecture experience are not substantiated
